filter_index.video_title: Why Kanamo Peak Is The Ultimate Himalayan Summit Adventure! | Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: once snow sets in after mid-October, region cut off until next summer
Spiti lies in rain shadow of Pir Panjal range; no heavy rains like other parts of Himachal during monsoon/post-monsoon

terrain_challenges: Steep ascents and descents on uneven trails
Boulder sections with scree and moraine
No vegetation cover above 15,000 ft - continuous open landscape
Relentless climb over scree, boulder and moraine from base camp to summit
Arduous terrain on summit day covering ~10 km in 12-14 hours
altitude_or_weather_risks: Trek begins at 13,500 ft and climbs to 19,500 ft - the highest Indiahikes goes on any trek in India
Risk of altitude sickness (AMS) especially in the first couple of days due to starting high and ascending further
AMS can affect anyone - first-time and experienced trekkers alike; even fit people climbing too high too soon are at equal risk
At altitudes above 10,000 ft, chances of Acute Mountain Sickness are real
Sunny days: direct harsh sunlight exposure can cause heat exhaustion; hydration is critical

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 10 km in under 60 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
Cardiovascular endurance and muscle strength are the two areas to work on

Strength training includes 60 standard pushups (or alternatives: 4x25=100 knee pushups or 4x50=200 wall pushups), 80 full squats (or 4x40=160 half squats), jumping lunges (or forward lunges), and planks (split into halves if needed)
approval_requirements: Every trekker needs fitness approval from the Indiahikes team; without it you will not be allowed on the trek
Record at least 3 runs from the last 1 month that meet the fitness criteria
Fitness record may be rejected if you fail to link your fitness profile or do not meet the required fitness level at least three times in the past month
prep_window: 6-week plan to get trek-fit if not currently active
Start the fitness routine 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins

experience_themes: once snow sets in after mid-October, region cut off until next summer
primary_watchouts: water sources begin to dry up after mid-October
region gets cut off once snow sets in
Steep ascents and descents on uneven trails
Boulder sections with scree and moraine
No vegetation cover above 15,000 ft - continuous open landscape
Relentless climb over scree, boulder and moraine from base camp to summit
not_ideal_for: First-time trekkers (altitude sickness risk applies to anyone, including first-timers)
Those without a high order of physical fitness
Those unable to handle relentless climbs over scree, boulder and moraine at altitude
